title: Akamai Guardicore Reveal Label Coverage and Ungrouped Assets (`guardicore:agent`)
spl:
index=microseg sourcetype="guardicore:agent" earliest=-4h
| where isnull(label_group) OR label_group=""
| stats dc(hostname) as unlabeled by datacenter
| where unlabeled>0
description: Assets without labels escape intent-based policies.
value: Microsegmentation program managers identify assets that have agents but lack labels, which means they escape intent-based policy enforcement and create gaps in the security posture.
implementation: Install the add-on, configure API polling intervals appropriate to API limits, and map assets to CMDB for enrichment lookups.

references.title: Splunkbase — Akamai Guardicore Add-on for Splunk

knownFalsePositives: Microseg blocks from new app deployments, missing exception rules, or planned segmentation rollouts.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Akamai Guardicore Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 7426) v3.x collecting agent and asset data from the Centra REST API.
• Data in `index=microseg` with `sourcetype=guardicore:agent`. Key fields: `hostname`, `ip_address`, `label_group`, `label_environment`, `label_application`, `label_role`, `datacenter`, `agent_status`.
• Guardicore's intent-based policies are driven by labels — assets without labels escape policy enforcement because no rule matches them. Label coverage is a prerequisite for effective microsegmentation.
• Labels in Guardicore follow a multi-dimensional model: Environment (prod/staging/dev), Application (app name), Role (web/app/db), and custom labels.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Verify label data is present:
```spl
index=microseg sourcetype="guardicore:agent" earliest=-4h
| stats count by label_group
| sort - count
```
Assets with `label_group=null` or `label_group=""` are ungrouped. These are the primary concern.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Primary search — Ungrouped asset identification:
```spl
index=microseg sourcetype="guardicore:agent" earliest=-4h
| where isnull(label_group) OR label_group="" OR label_group="Ungrouped"
| stats count as ungrouped_assets values(hostname) as hosts values(ip_address) as ips by datacenter
| appendpipe [
    search index=microseg sourcetype="guardicore:agent" earliest=-4h
    | stats dc(hostname) as total_assets
    | eval datacenter="TOTAL"
]
| sort datacenter
```

Understanding this SPL: Identifies assets that have a Guardicore agent but no label assignment. These assets are visible in Reveal (flow data is captured) but invisible to intent-based policies. In a mature deployment, ungrouped assets should be under 5% of total — anything higher indicates label hygiene problems.

Label completeness scoring:
```spl
index=microseg sourcetype="guardicore:agent" earliest=-4h
| eval has_env=if(isnotnull(label_environment) AND label_environment!="", 1, 0)
| eval has_app=if(isnotnull(label_application) AND label_application!="", 1, 0)
| eval has_role=if(isnotnull(label_role) AND label_role!="", 1, 0)
| eval label_score=has_env + has_app + has_role
| stats avg(label_score) as avg_score count(eval(label_score=3)) as fully_labeled count(eval(label_score=0)) as unlabeled dc(hostname) as total by datacenter
| eval fully_labeled_pct=round(fully_labeled/total*100, 1)
| sort - fully_labeled_pct
```

Step 3 — Validate
(a) In Guardicore Centra: Reveal > Labels. Compare the ungrouped asset count with Splunk.
(b) Pick 5 ungrouped assets from the search results and verify them in Centra — they should appear without label assignments.
(c) Cross-reference with CMDB: ungrouped assets often correlate with 'orphaned' VMs that have no documented owner.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard ("Guardicore — Label Coverage"):
• Row 1 — Single-value tiles: "Label coverage (%)", "Ungrouped assets", "Fully labeled assets".
• Row 2 — Bar chart: label completeness score by datacenter.
• Row 3 — Table: ungrouped asset list with hostname, IP, datacenter.

Alert: Weekly report on ungrouped assets. Trigger immediate alert if ungrouped count exceeds 10% of total assets.

Runbook:
1. **Newly provisioned VMs ungrouped** — Update the provisioning automation to apply labels at deployment time via the Centra API.
2. **Legacy assets ungrouped** — Manual labeling required. Work with the asset owner to determine the correct Environment/Application/Role labels.
3. **Assets with partial labels** — Missing Application or Role labels reduce policy precision. Complete the label set.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **Label data fields always null** — The TA may not be polling the asset/label API. Verify the TA's inputs include asset metadata collection.
• **Ungrouped count in Splunk doesn't match Centra** — Labels may have been applied after the last TA poll. Wait one poll cycle and re-check.
• **Label names inconsistent** — Standardize label naming conventions (e.g., 'Production' vs 'Prod' vs 'PROD'). Use Centra's bulk label management to normalize.
grandmaExplanation: We check that every computer in our network has been properly labeled and sorted into groups, because the ones that are not labeled slip through our security rules unnoticed.
